perturbation.original_text: All information, concerning the business of the other Party, hereafter in the possession of either of theParties, or their respective directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives, including, withoutlimitation, lawyers, accountants, consultants and financial advisors (collectively, "Representatives") andexcept as noted hereinafter is considered to be "Confidential Information".
perturbation.changed_text: All information, concerning the business of the other Party, hereafter in the possession of either of theParties, or their respective directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives is considered to be "Confidential Information".
perturbation.explanation: By removing "including, withoutlimitation, lawyers, accountants, consultants and financial advisors (collectively, "Representatives") andexcept as noted hereinafter", we create a contradiction because Section 1 and 2 refer to Representatives needing to keep the information confidential. Without this clause, it's unclear if lawyers, accountants, consultants and financial advisors are actually included under the definition of people need to keep confidential information, creating uncertainty in enforcement and contradicting sections that rely on the original definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. The Parties will not, and will direct their respective representatives and associates not to, disclose toany other person that the Confidential Information has been made available to them, that discussions ornegotiations are taking place concerning a potential Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or otherfacts with respect to any such Transaction. The term "person" used in this Agreement shall be broadlyinterpreted to include, without limitation, any individual, corporation, company, group partnership orother entity.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly includes 'lawyers, accountants, consultants and financial advisors (collectively, "Representatives")' within the definition of parties bound to confidentiality. The changed text removes this explicit inclusion. The contradicted text, Section 1, imposes obligations on 'representatives' regarding Confidential Information. By removing the explicit inclusion in the definition, the changed text creates uncertainty as to whether 'representatives' are still bound by the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 1, thus creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Furthermore, any specific product or venture ideas, know-how, or concepts which may be discussed aredeemed to be Confidential Information, provided that, (i) if in written or electronic form, they are clearlymarked "Confidential", or (ii) if otherwise uttered, they are at least verbally noted to the recipient Party bythe disclosing Party at the general time of disclosure as being confidential, and that subsequently they arenoted in writing within 7 calendar days of the time of disclosure to the recipient Party by the disclosingParty as being a confidential topic.
perturbation.changed_text: Furthermore, any specific product or venture ideas, know-how, or concepts which may be discussed aredeemed to be Confidential Information, provided that, (i) if in written or electronic form, they are clearlymarked "Confidential".
perturbation.explanation: By removing the condition regarding verbal disclosure and subsequent written confirmation for verbally disclosed information, only data that is in written or electronic form, and clearly marked "confidential" is now included within the definition. The ommission creates a contradiction because Section 3 mentions 'Information or concepts which are discussed', thus some can be verbal which is not 'confidential' anymore according to the modified definition. This creates an internal contradiction because the contract refers to the fact there might be verbal information considered confidential which the new definition does not contemplate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Information or concepts which are discussed by either Party as an extrapolation of the ConfidentialInformation disclosed are the property of the topic's disclosing Party. For greater clarity, the discussionor invention of an extension to Confidential Information does not give the uttering Party intellectualproperty rights to that aspect of the topic unless the related Confidential Information is theirs.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the condition regarding verbal disclosure and subsequent written confirmation for verbally disclosed information, meaning only written or electronic data marked "confidential" is now included. This contradicts Section 3, which refers to "Information or concepts which are discussed", implying some information can be verbal and was previously considered confidential. The modified definition no longer includes verbally disclosed information as confidential, creating an inconsistency with Section 3, as concepts discussed verbally are no longer automatically considered confidential, and the intellectual property rights are now unclear.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties will (and shall ensure that their respective Representatives will) keep the ConfidentialInformation confidential and will not, without the prior written consent of the other Party, disclose anyConfidential Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not use ConfidentialInformation, directly or indirectly, for other than evaluating a possible Transaction. The Parties shall Reciprocal Confidential Non-Disclosure Agreement 3.12 of 3make all reasonable, necessary or appropriate efforts to safeguard the Confidential Information fromdisclosure to anyone other than as permitted hereby.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will keep the ConfidentialInformation confidential and will not, without the prior written consent of the other Party, disclose anyConfidential Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not use ConfidentialInformation, directly or indirectly, for other than evaluating a possible Transaction. The Parties shall Reciprocal Confidential Non-Disclosure Agreement 3.12 of 3make all reasonable, necessary or appropriate efforts to safeguard the Confidential Information fromdisclosure to anyone other than as permitted hereby.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause "(and shall ensure that their respective Representatives will)" from Section 2, we introduce a contradiction with the 'DEFINITIONS' section, especially if the first perturbation is present (where it's unclear if Lawyers, Accountants, Consultants and Financial Advisors are included under the definition of people need to keep confidential information). Section 2 now only obligates the *Parties* to keep information confidential, but not explicitly their representatives. This creates a legal loophole and uncertainty about whether representatives are legally bound by the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All information, concerning the business of the other Party, hereafter in the possession of either of theParties, or their respective directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives is considered to be "Confidential Information".
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly includes representatives under the confidentiality obligation, whereas the changed text removes this obligation, only binding the Parties and not their representatives. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' as information in the possession of the Parties *or* their representatives. By removing the explicit mention of representatives' obligations in the changed text, a contradiction is created because the representatives are in possession of confidential information but are no longer explicitly bound by the confidentiality agreement, creating a legal loophole. This is because the DEFINITIONS section still includes representatives as holders of Confidential Information, but the main clause now only binds the Parties.

perturbation.original_text: If either of the Parties determines that it does not wish to be involved in a Transaction, it willpromptly advise the other Party of that fact, and if requested, each Party will promptly return to the otherParty all documents furnished hereunder to it or its respective Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: If either of the Parties determines that it does not wish to be involved in a Transaction, it willpromptly advise the other Party of that fact, and if requested, each Party will promptly return to the otherParty all documents furnished hereunder to it.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "or its respective Representatives" from Section 4, the obligation to return documents only applies to the Parties themselves, and not to their representatives. This contradicts earlier clauses that treat the Parties and their representatives as a single unit concerning confidentiality. If the Representatives retain documents, it's unclear if they are in breach of contract, creating an internal contradiction and uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties will keep the ConfidentialInformation confidential and will not, without the prior written consent of the other Party, disclose anyConfidential Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not use ConfidentialInformation, directly or indirectly, for other than evaluating a possible Transaction. The Parties shall Reciprocal Confidential Non-Disclosure Agreement 3.12 of 3make all reasonable, necessary or appropriate efforts to safeguard the Confidential Information fromdisclosure to anyone other than as permitted hereby.
perturbation.justification: The original text required both the Parties and their Representatives to return documents. The changed text removes 'or its respective Representatives,' limiting the return obligation to only the Parties. The Contradicted Text states that 'The Parties will keep the ConfidentialInformation confidential,' and 'The Parties shall make all reasonable, necessary or appropriate efforts to safeguard the Confidential Information fromdisclosure to anyone other than as permitted hereby'. By removing the Representatives from the obligation to return the documents, the parties are no longer responsible for their Representatives safeguarding the confidential information, which causes a direct contradiction with the Contradicted Text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it alters the scope of responsibility and could allow representatives to retain confidential information without breaching the modified clause, contradicting the clause requiring the parties to safeguard the confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Minutes of Confidential Information Disclosures.Dated: _____________________________, at ___________________________. During ameeting between_________________________________ of ______________________________, and_________________________________ of ______________________________, and_________________________________ of ______________________________, being eitherParties or representatives of Parties who are subject to a Reciprocal Non-disclosure Agreement,the following topics were claimed to be "Confidential Information".Confidential Information TopicDisclosed by Signatures as acknowledgements. Please sign, print and date.
perturbation.changed_text: Minutes of Information Disclosures.Dated: _____________________________, at ___________________________. During ameeting between_________________________________ of ______________________________, and_________________________________ of ______________________________, and_________________________________ of ______________________________, being eitherParties or representatives of Parties,the following topics were claimed to be "Confidential Information".Confidential Information TopicDisclosed by Signatures as acknowledgements. Please sign, print and date.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "who are subject to a Reciprocal Non-disclosure Agreement", from the last section, it contradicts the very purpose of the NDA. It is no longer clearly defined that attendees, either Parties or representatives are subject to a Reciprocal Non-disclosure Agreement. This creates an internal contradiction because it undermines the foundational principle of the contract, which is to maintain confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All information, concerning the business of the other Party, hereafter in the possession of either of theParties, or their respective directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives is considered to be "Confidential Information".
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the attendees of the meeting are subject to a Reciprocal Non-disclosure Agreement. The changed text removes this specification, creating a contradiction with the Contradicted Text, which assumes that all information concerning the business of the other party is considered "Confidential Information". Without the specification that attendees are subject to the Reciprocal Non-disclosure Agreement, there's no clear obligation for them to treat the disclosed information as confidential, directly conflicting with the definition of 'Confidential Information' in the Contradicted Text. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction, as essential language has been removed in one section, creating a contradiction with constraints stated in other sections.
